title: Benzoyl-m-Nitro-Acetanilide as A Gravimetric Reagent for Cadmium
author: PRADIP KUMAR DAN
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, The University of Burdwan, Burdwan 713101 

  Manuscript received 27 August 1976, revised 21 January 1977, accepted 18 March 1977  

 Cadmium (14-23 mg) has been quantitatively precipitated with benzoyl-m-nitro­acetanilide and the highly insoluble yellow-coloured bis complex has been dried at 110° for weighing. The conversion factor is 0.1656. The relative standard deviation is ±0.14%. Separation from Ni 2+ , Co 2+ , Zn 2+ , Mn 2+ , Pb 2 +, Mg 2+ , Al 2+ , In 2+  and Cr 2+  was possible using sodium or magnesium-EDTA as the masking agent. Thermogravi­metric studies indicated the complex to be stable upto 410°.
